codeReview.summary: The feature delivers the new batch movement endpoint, raw-material movement linkage into production logs, packing linkage into production detail DTOs, and low-stock threshold APIs with focused tests and gate-fast evidence. However, there is a blocking functional gap against the batch-tracking requirement: raw-material batch metadata does not ingest manufacturing/expiry dates in purchase/intake paths, so those fields are not reliably tracked for every raw-material batch as specified.

issues.description: RawMaterialBatchRequest does not expose manufacturingDate or expiryDate inputs, making it impossible for goods-receipt/intake flows to capture those required batch-traceability fields.

recordReceipt hard-sets manufacturedAt to now and never sets expiryDate from caller data, so purchased raw-material batches cannot preserve actual manufacturing/expiry metadata required by the feature contract.

Opening stock import parses manufacturedDate but has no expiry-date field in OpeningRow and no expiry assignment for imported raw-material or finished-good batches, leaving required batch expiry tracking incomplete.
Requirement 'ensure every inventory batch (raw material and finished good) tracks ... manufacturing date, expiry date' is not fully met for raw-material purchase/intake/opening-stock flows.
issues.evidence: RawMaterialBatchRequest.java:8-15 has no manufactured/expiry fields; RawMaterialService.java:272 sets manufacturedAt to current time and never sets expiryDate; PurchasingService.java:444-450 constructs RawMaterialBatchRequest with only batchCode/quantity/unit/cost/supplier/notes; OpeningStockImportService.java:726 defines only manufacturedDate in OpeningRow and no expiry parsing or assignment.

issues.description: Low-stock thresholds are persisted and configurable via dedicated endpoints, but they are not surfaced in the main FinishedGoodDto payload.
issues.evidence: FinishedGoodsWorkflowService.java:199-217 implements get/update threshold and resolveLowStockThreshold; FinishedGoodDto.java:6-18 does not include lowStockThreshold, so clients must call separate threshold endpoint to display/edit this value.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission guidance and worker skill instructions conflict about .factory modifications.
sharedStateObservations.rationale: AGENTS.md forbids modifying .factory mission infrastructure, while backend-worker skill mandates updating .factory/library/frontend-handoff.md for API changes; this inconsistency can cause procedural drift and confusion for workers performing API features.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker transcript shows routine use of .factory/init.sh but services.yaml does not define an init/setup command.
sharedStateObservations.rationale: Standardizing this command in services.yaml would improve reproducibility for workers and reviewers and reduce ad-hoc setup invocation patterns.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: worker-transcripts.jsonl entry for session cdb634ba-a46a-4031-969a-ac673a42d835 includes Execute '/home/realnigga/Desktop/Mission-control/.factory/init.sh'; /home/realnigga/Desktop/Mission-control/.factory/services.yaml lists compile/test/build/lint but no init command

summary: Review result: fail. Most traceability and stock-linkage behaviors are implemented (movement history endpoint, production material movement linkage, packing-to-production linkage, and per-product low-stock threshold APIs), but a blocking gap remains because required manufacturing/expiry metadata is not captured for raw-material batches across key intake flows, so the 'every batch tracks manufacturing+expiry' contract is not fully satisfied.
